2026-06-09.patch_summary: This patch addresses a use-after-free bug in the lockd and nfsd modules by RCU-protecting the nlmsvc_ops dispatch. The bug occurs when the nfsd module is removed, causing stale loads of the nlmsvc_ops pointer. To fix this, the patch declares nlmsvc_ops as __rcu, publishes it via rcu_assign_pointer(), and clears it via RCU_INIT_POINTER() + synchronize_rcu(). Additionally, a struct module *owner field is added to nlmsvc_binding and the module is pinned across indirect calls using try_module_get/module_put. This ensures that the binding is properly torn down when the module is removed, avoiding leaks of struct file references.
